author: tersec
content:
I could try, but if possible I'd be just as happy to access whatever datastore might hold these, copy the logs, etc, and go from there. So far I've kept my LLM access set up with 0 credentials to anything in its own isolated space, so its blast radius is... well, I guess, the Internet. But nothing private

I've looked for old Docker logs before and I have no idea if they're even preserved

author: parithosh
content:
panda spins up a sandbox container to do anything, so your blast radius is kinda just the sandbox container. it can't access the host to do anything. it uses github oauth the to authenticate against the datasources. https://ethpandaops.io/posts/panda/ (sandbox section of the post tells you what permissions the sandbox is configured with)

You can also use panda as a standalone cli and use it to just return logs for you, but its gonna be a clunkier way to use it. 

but if you want to avoid the cli altogether, you can - the logs are visible in grafana and or via ssh + docker logs  😄

author: qu0b
content: Just ask the LLM for the right panda CLI commands and run them yourself 🙂

author: tersec
content: well, at any given time the docker logs cover 10-20 minutes as far as I can tell

author: tersec
content: unless there are more elsewhere that `docker logs` doesn't show. I already use that

author: parithosh
content:
that doesnt sound right, docker logs should have a much longer timeframe - its usually limimted by the size it takes up on the host and we used to set it at like 500mb or something relatively high.

if not, they will be visible in grafana under the explore tab, choose clickhouse logs as the option

author: qu0b
content: the amount of logs is pretty high 😅

author: qu0b
content:
```
panda clickhouse query clickhouse-raw "SELECT Timestamp, SeverityText, Body FROM external.otel_logs WHERE ServiceName='beacon' AND ResourceAttributes['network']='glamsterdam-devnet-5' AND ResourceAttributes['host.name']='nimbus-erigon-1' AND Timestamp > now() - INTERVAL 1 HOUR ORDER BY Timestamp DESC LIMIT 100"
```

content: <@654267572107083777> you can switch out host.name `nimbus-erigon-1` with `nimbus-nethermind-1`

author: nflaig
content: <@755590043632140352> I am stealing your idea https://github.com/ChainSafe/lodestar/issues/9470

author: potuz
content: Yeah I think it can probably help mitigate other bugs like the peering issue we seem to have and still can't solve

author: nflaig
content: I had this idea yesterday and wanted to bring it up, but then today saw you already have a PR for it on Prysm

author: nflaig
content:
although what lodestar already does seem smarter and better than what you do in your PR, we will request the paylad if
- (1) we receive an attestation for block root with with `index == 1`
- (2) or if we receive a ptc attestation for block root with `payload_present == True`
so if clients do their ptc attestation asap then (2) might be sufficient/ideal

author: terencechain
content:
another thing is do you request both block and payload in parallel if you don't have the block 
Or do you get the block first then request for the payload

author: nflaig
content:
to add to list above, we also request payloads if
- (3) if we receive a `data_column_sidecar` for payload
- (4) we receive a `execution_payload_bid` for slot N+1 whose parent payload (slot N) we're missing
- (5) `beacon_block` that builds on a unknown payload
but for the scenario we had here, (2) would have been most effective

author: nflaig
content: parallel

author: potuz
content: We already do this. But that's useless for same slot

author: nflaig
content: why is (2) useless, lodestar sent our `payload_attestation_message` at ~1 second into the slot as we received the payload really early

author: potuz
content: I meant for 1

author: potuz
content: For 2 it would be an abuse

author: potuz
content: Cause the proposer and first nodes will send those PTC attestations

author: potuz
content: You shouldn't make RPC requests for objects you expect to get soon

author: nflaig
content: that's right, but a grace period/delay should easily solve this

author: potuz
content: Problem is that we're going with tight numbers for broadcast so by the time you get an attestation, if your grace period is enough for propagation, you get to the Payload timelines deadline anyway

author: nflaig
content: this might be the case, so your idea is to just use `PAYLOAD_DUE_BPS`, meaning if you don't receive the payload timely over gossip you don't ever want your ptc to vote timely?

author: potuz
content: Yes, in general some parts of 7732 are just there because we need to protect from dishonest behavior that will not happen anyway. So for example I don't care at all about proposer preferences and I think we would be fine without them.  PTC is something we need, but it's not that crucial, nothing will happen if no PTC member is online and the next proposer is honest. So the reason to request protectively the Payload is to protect the next proposer/attester to not miss its block/attestation because they didn't get a Payload that everyone did
